title: Ships as installable Claude Code skill
detail: Has SKILL.md (27KB), `notebooklm skill install` CLI, and `npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py` distribution. Directly relevant to JASON-OS skill portability goals.
evidence: README sections: 'Agent Setup' — CLI install writes to ~/.claude/skills/notebooklm and ~/.agents/skills/notebooklm.

title: Three-surface architecture from one codebase
detail: Single Python package exposes Python SDK, CLI, and agentic skill. README calls these 'Three Ways to Use'.

title: Test workflow 45% failure rate
detail: Main Test workflow fails 9/20 recent runs while Nightly E2E, CodeQL, Verify, and RPC Health all pass 100%. Suggests tolerated flakiness in unit test suite.

title: No branch protection on main
detail: GraphQL branchProtectionRules returns empty. Solo maintainer with direct push.

title: Bus factor ~1
detail: teng-lin has 551 commits; #2 contributor has 21. Dependabot bot is #5.

title: Uses undocumented Google APIs
detail: Disclosed prominently in README as unofficial. Risk: APIs can break anytime. Not a defect, but relevant to adoption.
evidence: README warning block: 'Unofficial Library - Use at Your Own Risk'.

title: Agent-facing docs are first-class
detail: Root contains CLAUDE.md (7.6KB), AGENTS.md (2.2KB), SKILL.md (26.8KB), plus SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING/CHANGELOG. Every major audience (Claude Code, Codex, human contributor) has a dedicated entry-point doc.
evidence: Contents API listing of root directory.

title: Viral trajectory
detail: 9228 stars + 1184 forks in 88 days, Trendshift-featured. 12.8% fork-to-star ratio indicates users are building on it, not just bookmarking.

title: References 'open skills ecosystem'
detail: `npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py` fetches SKILL.md from GitHub via an unnamed 'open skills ecosystem'. Potentially relevant to JASON-OS distribution model.
